project.name: Apache Kafka
project.description: Open-source distributed event streaming platform for high-performance data pipelines, streaming analytics, data integration, and mission-critical applications.

nodes.name: Event Streaming & Pub-Sub
nodes.summary: Core publish-subscribe messaging system enabling producers to publish events to topics and consumers to subscribe, handling topic partitioning, message ordering, and offset management.

businessRules: Messages in a partition are ordered sequentially
Consumers track offset to resume from last processed message
Multiple consumer groups can consume same topic independently
Topic deletion is irreversible without recovery
crossDomainInteractions: Replication ensures message durability
Coordination manages consumer group state
Client APIs enable producer/consumer access
Metadata management provides topic/partition information

nodes.name: Replication & Durability
nodes.summary: Ensures message durability and availability through leader-follower replication across brokers, in-sync replica (ISR) management, and log storage.

businessRules: At least one ISR must exist for acknowledged writes
Leader handles all reads and writes
Followers replicate data asynchronously
Broker failures trigger leader election for affected partitions
Committed log is persisted to disk for durability
crossDomainInteractions: Distributed coordination elects partition leaders
Event streaming depends on replicated data
Metadata management tracks replica assignments

nodes.name: Distributed Coordination & Metadata
nodes.summary: Manages cluster topology, controller election, broker registration, metadata versioning via KRaft consensus, and replication factor enforcement.

businessRules: Single active controller at any time (leadership via quorum)
All metadata changes flow through controller
Replica assignments define durability requirements
Broker registration triggers metadata updates
Metadata versioning enables incremental sync
crossDomainInteractions: Enables leader election for event streaming partitions
Manages broker lifecycle and cluster changes
Provides configuration for replication policies
Coordinates consumer group changes

nodes.name: Stream Processing
nodes.summary: Provides topology-based stream processing allowing applications to transform, aggregate, and analyze event streams with stateful operations and processor trees.

businessRules: Topology defines processing DAG before execution
Each task processes events from assigned partitions
State stores maintain local materialized state
Exactly-once semantics require offset commit coordination
Multi-threaded execution improves throughput
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes from event streaming topics as sources
Produces results to event streaming topics as sinks
Uses client APIs for internal communication
Leverages coordination for consumer group management

nodes.name: Data Integration via Connect
nodes.summary: Integrates external systems with Kafka through pluggable source and sink connectors, enabling data pipelines between Kafka and databases, APIs, file systems.

businessRules: Connectors run as distributed tasks across worker nodes
Source connectors poll external systems and produce to Kafka
Sink connectors consume from Kafka and write to external systems
Offsets track source/sink progress for exactly-once semantics
Configuration is stored in Kafka topics for durability
crossDomainInteractions: Uses event streaming for configuration and offset topics
Uses coordination for connector task assignment
Uses client APIs for internal communication

nodes.name: Client Communication & Protocols
nodes.summary: Manages network communication between clients and brokers via Kafka protocol, handling request/response routing, authentication, TLS encryption, and API versioning.

businessRules: Binary protocol enables efficient serialization
API versioning ensures backward compatibility
TLS provides encryption and authentication
Rate limiting prevents resource exhaustion
Connection pooling minimizes latency
crossDomainInteractions: Handles requests from all client types
Routes to appropriate domain handlers
Enforces security and quotas

nodes.name: Message Production
nodes.summary: Producer sends messages to topics, which are routed to appropriate partitions, replicated across brokers, and acknowledged to client.

nodes.name: Validate Producer Request
nodes.summary: Validate message format, topic existence, authorization, and quotas

nodes.name: Route to Partition
nodes.summary: Determine target partition using partitioner or explicit partition assignment

nodes.name: Append to Leader Log
nodes.summary: Broker leader appends messages to partition log segment in sequential order

nodes.name: Replicate to Followers
nodes.summary: Followers asynchronously fetch and apply log entries to maintain consistency

nodes.name: Send Acknowledgment
nodes.summary: Broker sends ProduceResponse with offset and timestamp to producer

nodes.name: Message Consumption
nodes.summary: Consumer fetches messages from topics/partitions, updates offset position, handles group coordination and rebalancing.

nodes.name: Join Consumer Group
nodes.summary: Register consumer with group coordinator and participate in group rebalancing

nodes.name: Assign Partitions
nodes.summary: Coordinator assigns partitions to consumer using rebalancing strategy

nodes.name: Seek to Offset
nodes.summary: Consumer seeks to last committed offset or specified position

nodes.name: Fetch Records from Broker
nodes.summary: Broker services fetch request by reading log segments from assigned partitions

nodes.name: Commit Offset
nodes.summary: Consumer commits current offset to consumer group coordinator for durability

nodes.name: Send Heartbeat
nodes.summary: Consumer sends periodic heartbeat to remain active in group and detect failures

nodes.name: Leader-Follower Replication
nodes.summary: Synchronizes message replicas across brokers using fetch-based replication, maintaining ISR for durability guarantees.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Replica assignment or leader election event

nodes.name: Leader Appends Message
nodes.summary: Leader writes message to its log and marks as uncommitted

nodes.name: Follower Fetches from Leader
nodes.summary: Replica fetcher thread periodically fetches new entries from leader partition

nodes.name: Follower Appends to Log
nodes.summary: Follower writes fetched entries to its log in same order

nodes.name: Update ISR Status
nodes.summary: Leader tracks which replicas are in-sync based on fetch lag and heartbeats

nodes.name: Mark as Committed
nodes.summary: Leader marks message as committed when ISR threshold is met and notifies followers

nodes.name: Broker Registration & Discovery
nodes.summary: Broker registers with controller, announces capabilities/configuration, and maintains active membership via heartbeats.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Broker startup or periodic heartbeat event

nodes.name: Connect to Controller
nodes.summary: Broker discovers and connects to active controller node

nodes.name: Send Registration Request
nodes.summary: Broker sends BrokerRegistration with ID, epoch, endpoints, and configuration

nodes.name: Receive MetadataImage
nodes.summary: Controller sends back current cluster metadata snapshot to broker

nodes.name: Apply Local Metadata
nodes.summary: Broker integrates metadata into local state and triggers necessary operations (partition startup)

nodes.name: Controller Leadership Election
nodes.summary: Establishes single controller via KRaft quorum consensus, handles handoff, and propagates leadership changes.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Controller startup or leader failure detection

nodes.name: Participate in Quorum Vote
nodes.summary: Controller node requests votes from quorum members during election

nodes.name: Acquire Leadership
nodes.summary: Winning node transitions to leader state after majority quorum confirms

nodes.name: Broadcast Controller Epoch
nodes.summary: Controller broadcasts new epoch to all brokers to coordinate leadership change

nodes.name: Stream Topology Execution
nodes.summary: Initializes stream application topology, assigns tasks to threads, and processes events through processor pipeline.

nodes.name: Build Topology
nodes.summary: StreamsBuilder creates processor topology DAG from source/sink/state definitions

nodes.name: Assign Partition Tasks
nodes.summary: TaskManager assigns partitions to local tasks and creates processor instances

nodes.name: Initialize State Stores
nodes.summary: Open and restore state stores from changelog topics or local cache

nodes.name: Start Stream Threads
nodes.summary: Create worker threads that poll from assigned partitions and process records

nodes.name: Process Records Through Topology
nodes.summary: Stream thread fetches records and routes through processor chain (filters, maps, joins, aggregates)

nodes.name: Commit Offsets Periodically
nodes.summary: Stream thread commits processed record offsets to coordinator for exactly-once semantics

nodes.name: Data Integration via Connector
nodes.summary: Executes data pipeline where source connector polls external system, produces to Kafka topic, or sink connector consumes and writes out.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Worker task assignment or connector configuration

nodes.name: Source Polls External System
nodes.summary: Source connector queries external system (database, API, file) for new data

nodes.name: Source Produces to Kafka
nodes.summary: Source connector transforms external records to SourceRecords and produces to topic

nodes.name: Sink Consumes from Kafka
nodes.summary: Sink connector consumer group fetches records from assigned topic partitions

nodes.name: Sink Transforms Records
nodes.summary: Sink connector prepares records for external system (format conversion, schema mapping)

nodes.name: Sink Writes to External System
nodes.summary: Sink connector writes transformed records to external system (database, file, API)

nodes.name: Network Request-Response Handling
nodes.summary: Receives client requests on socket server, routes to appropriate handler, processes, and sends response with error handling.

nodes.name: Accept Socket Connection
nodes.summary: SocketServer accepts incoming TCP connection from client

nodes.name: Parse Request Bytes
nodes.summary: NetworkProcessor deserializes binary request into KafkaRequest object

nodes.name: Authenticate Client
nodes.summary: Verify client credentials (SASL/SSL) and authorize request against ACLs

nodes.name: Dispatch to Handler
nodes.summary: KafkaApis routes request to appropriate handler based on API type (Produce, Fetch, Metadata, etc)

nodes.name: Send Response
nodes.summary: Serialize KafkaResponse back to binary and send to client socket

nodes.description: Event streaming depends on replicated storage for durability guarantees
Distributed coordination provides topic/partition metadata and broker endpoints
Client communication enables producer/consumer network requests
Distributed coordination assigns replicas and elects partition leaders
Stream processing reads from and writes to event streaming topics
Distributed coordination manages consumer group state for stream applications
Kafka Connect integrates external systems via event streaming topics
Distributed coordination assigns connector tasks to workers and manages config
All event streaming operations flow through network communication layer

edges.description: Event streaming depends on replicated storage for durability guarantees
Distributed coordination provides topic/partition metadata and broker endpoints
Client communication enables producer/consumer network requests
Distributed coordination assigns replicas and elects partition leaders
Stream processing reads from and writes to event streaming topics
Distributed coordination manages consumer group state for stream applications
Kafka Connect integrates external systems via event streaming topics
Distributed coordination assigns connector tasks to workers and manages config
All event streaming operations flow through network communication layer
